---
title: "03_differential_expression"
format: html
---

```{python}
data_filtered = pd.read_csv("data_filtered.csv", index_col=0)
sample = pd.read_csv("sample.csv", index_col=0)
genes_metadata = pd.read_csv("tcga_brca_gene_metadata.csv")
```

```{python}
sample.head()
```

## Applying DESeq2

DESeq2 takes RNA-seq count data and identifies genes whose expression is significantly different between conditions.

```{python}
#change the column name to condition
condition_data = sample[["tcga.cgc_sample_sample_type"]].copy()
condition_data  = condition_data .rename(columns={"tcga.cgc_sample_sample_type": "condition"})

# Map the long TCGA labels to short, consistent values
condition_data ["condition"] = condition_data ["condition"].map({
    "Primary Tumor": "cancer",
    "Solid Tissue Normal": "normal"
})

# Confirm mapping worked and nothing became NaN
print(condition_data ["condition"].value_counts(dropna=False))

# Set "normal" as the reference level, so fold changes read as tumor-vs-normal
condition_data ["condition"] = condition_data ["condition"].astype("category")
condition_data ["condition"] = condition_data ["condition"].cat.reorder_categories(["normal", "cancer"])
```

```{python}
condition_data.shape
```

```{python}
data_filtered = data_filtered.apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce")
```

```{python}
from pydeseq2.dds import DeseqDataSet
from pydeseq2.ds import DeseqStats

counts_for_dds = data_filtered.T
dds = DeseqDataSet(
    counts=counts_for_dds,
    metadata=condition_data,
    design_factors="condition",
    refit_cooks=True,
)

dds.deseq2()
```

```{python}
stat_res = DeseqStats(
    dds,
    contrast=["condition", "cancer", "normal"],  # explicit: cancer vs. normal
)
stat_res.summary()
 
results_df = stat_res.results_df.sort_values("padj")
 
print(results_df.head(10))
```

**Standard filtering for "significant" genes**, typically: `padj < 0.05` and `|log2FoldChange| > 1`. This combination is what most published cancer RNA-seq papers use as their significance bar,

```{python}
results_df.to_csv("deseq2_results_tcga_brca.csv")
sig_genes = results_df[
(results_df["padj"]< 0.05) &
(results_df["log2FoldChange"].abs() > 1)]

print(f"Total genes tested: {len(results_df)}")
print(f"Significant genes (padj<0.05, |log2FC|>1): {len(sig_genes)}")
```

Mapping genes

```{python}
genes_metadata = genes_metadata.set_index("gene_id")
```

```{python}
print(sig_genes.index[:3])
print(genes_metadata.index[:3])
```

```{python}
#add new column for gene symol and map it with genes metadata
sig_genes["gene_symbol"] = sig_genes.index.map(genes_metadata["gene_name"])
sig_genes.head()
```

```{python}
# Split into up- and down-regulated 
up_in_cancer = sig_genes[sig_genes["log2FoldChange"] > 1]
down_in_cancer = sig_genes[sig_genes["log2FoldChange"] < -1]

print(f" Up in Cancer: {len(up_in_cancer)}")
print(f" Down in Cancer: {len(down_in_cancer)}")
```

```{python}
# Look at them, sorted by strength of change not just significance
print("Top 10 upregulated in cancer (by fold change):")
print(up_in_cancer.sort_values("log2FoldChange", ascending=False).head(10))
 
print("\nTop 10 downregulated in cancer (by fold change):")
print(down_in_cancer.sort_values("log2FoldChange").head(10))
```

```{python}
sig_genes.to_csv("significant_genes.csv")
up_in_cancer.to_csv("upregulated_genes.csv")
down_in_cancer.to_csv("downregulated_genes.csv")
```
